title: dactylorhiza affinis
intro: Dactylorhiza affinis, also known as the autumn lady's tresses, is a species of orchid that belongs to the Dactylorhiza genus. This perennial plant is native to Europe and Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza affinis include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, Dactylorhiza affinis can be found in various forms, including tinctures, infusions, and dried root extracts, which can be used to make teas, capsules, and topical creams.
